## 🛠️ FEATURED PRODUCTS

Each project reflects a journey of solving a unique problem using specialized technologies. Click on the cards to explore the repositories.

<table width="100%" border="0" cellpadding="5" cellspacing="0">
  <!-- PROJECT 1: MINDMIRROR -->
  <tr>
    <td style="border: none; padding-bottom: 20px;">
      <a href="https://github.com/soham-arch/MindMirror" target="_blank">
        <img src="assets/svg/project_cards/mindmirror_card.svg" alt="MindMirror Card" width="100%" />
      </a>
      <details>
        <summary><b>🔍 Technical Deep-Dive: MindMirror</b> (Click to expand)</summary>
        <br/>
        <blockquote>
          <b>The Problem:</b> Mental well-being diaries are tedious to maintain, and users rarely notice emotional trends or harmful cognitive distortion patterns without professional analysis.<br/>
          <b>The Solution:</b> An intelligent journaling platform that uses NLP to extract semantic sentiment, logs psychological distortions (e.g., catastrophizing), and generates visual dashboard summaries of mental health health state.<br/>
          <b>Tech Stack:</b> React, Node.js, Python (NLTK/Transformers), Tailwind CSS, Firebase Firestore.<br/>
          <b>Current Status:</b> Active refinement. Planning to integrate voice-based journaling with multi-modal tone analysis.
        </blockquote>
      </details>
    </td>
  </tr>
  
  <!-- PROJECT 2: CRIMEFACE -->
  <tr>
    <td style="border: none; padding-bottom: 20px;">
      <a href="https://github.com/soham-arch/CrimeFace" target="_blank">
        <img src="assets/svg/project_cards/crimeface_card.svg" alt="CrimeFace Card" width="100%" />
      </a>
      <details>
        <summary><b>🔍 Technical Deep-Dive: CrimeFace</b> (Click to expand)</summary>
        <br/>
        <blockquote>
          <b>The Problem:</b> Video surveillance identification is manual and extremely slow when cross-referencing multi-million record criminal suspect files.<br/>
          <b>The Solution:</b> A low-latency, deep learning-powered facial identification pipeline. Converts CCTV face feeds to high-dimensional embeddings and executes sub-millisecond vector similarity search against a Milvus index.<br/>
          <b>Tech Stack:</b> Python, OpenCV, PyTorch, FastAPI, Milvus, Docker.<br/>
          <b>Current Status:</b> Production-ready. Currently improving occlusion robustness and multi-camera person re-identification.
        </blockquote>
      </details>
    </td>
  </tr>

  <!-- PROJECT 3: TURFLY -->
  <tr>
    <td style="border: none; padding-bottom: 20px;">
      <a href="https://github.com/soham-arch/Turfly" target="_blank">
        <img src="assets/svg/project_cards/turfly_card.svg" alt="Turfly Card" width="100%" />
      </a>
      <details>
        <summary><b>🔍 Technical Deep-Dive: Turfly</b> (Click to expand)</summary>
        <br/>
        <blockquote>
          <b>The Problem:</b> Sports enthusiasts experience friction booking turf courts and assembling enough local players to form matches.<br/>
          <b>The Solution:</b> A mobile booking platform that integrates real-time scheduling APIs with a social matchmaking engine where players can host or join public matches.<br/>
          <b>Tech Stack:</b> React Native, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, Google Maps API.<br/>
          <b>Current Status:</b> Deployment phase. Moving to integrate league tournament brackets and group split-payment gateways.
        </blockquote>
      </details>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>

## 🎯 DEVELOPER PHILOSOPHY & ROADMAP GOALS

<table width="100%" border="0" cellpadding="10" cellspacing="0">
  <tr>
    <td width="50%" valign="top" style="border: none; border-right: 1px solid #1e293b;">
      <h3 align="center">💡 Engineering Philosophy</h3>
      <p align="left">
        <strong>1. Build with Purpose:</strong> Coding is a superpower. Every line of code should be aimed at solving a specific friction point or creating concrete utility.
      </p>
      <p align="left">
        <strong>2. Strive for Simplicity:</strong> Simple architectures scale better, are easier to debug, and survive user growth. Over-engineering is a debt paid in production.
      </p>
      <p align="left">
        <strong>3. Stay Adaptable:</strong> The AI field changes weekly. A great engineer is not defined by their current stack, but by their speed of adapting to new paradigms.
      </p>
    </td>
    <td width="50%" valign="top" style="border: none;">
      <h3 align="center">🎯 Future Milestones</h3>
      <ul>
        <li><b>Open Source:</b> Commit feature integrations to LlamaIndex/LangChain/AutoGen core libraries.</li>
        <li><b>Vector DB Architectures:</b> Implement raw C++ extensions for custom distance metrics in vector search.</li>
        <li><b>Micro-SaaS Incubator:</b> Ship, launch, and validate two AI products to 500+ active users.</li>
        <li><b>Mentorship & Sharing:</b> Write in-depth articles detailing RAG optimization and agentic behaviors.</li>
      </ul>
    </td>
  </tr>
</table>
